nodes.label: AI as content generator for engaging examples
nodes.label_negative: AI as source of irrelevant or unengaging content
nodes.grounding_span: I often use AI to find examples for things I'm teaching e.g. I might be teaching biodiversity and want some unique real world examples to keep the students engaged

nodes.label: AI as collaborative problem generator and checker
nodes.label_negative: AI as unreliable or incorrect checker
nodes.grounding_span: I could use the AI to help me generate similar but different problems and provide me wth solutions to check my own answers. If the answers differed to mine, I would then review my own answer (although sometimes the AI is wrong!)

nodes.label: AI as time-saving tool for administrative tasks
nodes.label_negative: AI as time-consuming or inefficient tool
nodes.grounding_span: Saving time e.g. asking it to make a spreadsheet from loads of handwritten note about university destinations of students on Alevel results day

nodes.label: AI as personalized guidance assistant for student admissions
nodes.label_negative: AI as impersonal or inaccurate guidance tool
nodes.grounding_span: I've started to use it to help gather admissions data about UK universities e.g. a student could tell me what grades they have... and the AI can rank which medical schools might be the best fit

nodes.label: Human-only domain for student mentoring and relationship
nodes.grounding_span: Yes, that would be talking to / mentoring students

nodes.label: AI as future eliminator of mundane tasks enabling focus on personalization
nodes.label_negative: AI as threat to jobs for future generations
nodes.grounding_span: I will use it more and more and it will eliminate some of the more mundane tasks so that I can focus on personalising learning more

nodes.label: Teaching accuracy and reliability
nodes.grounding_span: provide me wth solutions to check my own answers

nodes.label: Human relationship and trust in mentoring
nodes.grounding_span: I've built up a relationship with the students

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI as time-saver
nodes.grounding_span: Saving time e.g. asking it to make a spreadsheet from loads of handwritten note about university destinations of students on Alevel results day

nodes.label: Cautious trust in AI as checker (aware of errors)
nodes.grounding_span: If the answers differed to mine, I would then review my own answer (although sometimes the AI is wrong!)

nodes.label: Optimistic anticipation of increased AI use for personalization
nodes.grounding_span: I will use it more and more and it will eliminate some of the more mundane tasks so that I can focus on personalising learning more

nodes.label: Concern about AI replacing jobs for future generations
nodes.grounding_span: It concerns me more for my childrens generation than my own

nodes.label: Experiential filtering — uses classroom experience to curate AI output
nodes.grounding_span: I'm very experienced in the classroom, so I know what will engage the students

nodes.label: Collaborative iterative refinement — works with AI in back-and-forth manner
nodes.grounding_span: I tend to collaborate as I go
